title: Shutdown

jokes.text: Did I hear the VP scream? No, that was me. And it was a shout.
jokes.setup: Someone heard what sounded like the VP screaming
jokes.punchline: Gary defensively correcting that he shouted, not screamed, while admitting it was him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary character moment - his wounded pride over being mistaken for the VP while also being embarrassed about screaming at a rat

jokes.text: That was a man's voice? Yeah, 'cause I'm a man. I have a man's voice.
jokes.setup: Someone questions if that high-pitched scream was a man's voice
jokes.punchline: Gary's increasingly desperate assertion of his masculinity
jokes.explanation: Classic Gary insecurity manifesting as overcompensation about his masculinity

jokes.text: Keith stays, right? What? Keith. He's skinny like a skeleton.
jokes.setup: Selina mentions needing a skeleton staff during shutdown
jokes.punchline: Someone misunderstanding 'skeleton staff' literally, suggesting Keith because he's skinny
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay joke that gets a mild laugh but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: Gary, the Secret Service is calling you Girly Shirley Temple.
jokes.setup: Gary's been trying to assert his masculinity
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Secret Service has given him an emasculating nickname
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that escalates Gary's masculinity crisis with a specific, humiliating detail

jokes.text: Sue crying and saying 'I need a moment' when furloughed
jokes.setup: Sue, typically composed, gets furloughed
jokes.punchline: Her unexpected emotional breakdown
jokes.explanation: Good character subversion - showing Sue's human side when her professional composure cracks

jokes.text: He's like Spock. He doesn't show his emotions. Ever? Ever? No, not even then.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Dan's emotional detachment
jokes.punchline: The increasingly specific denial that he shows emotion even during sex
jokes.explanation: Well-structured escalation that builds from general observation to intimate specifics

jokes.text: Dan only laughed when security guy fell off Segway, followed by 'Is laughter an emotion?'
jokes.setup: Discussion of Dan's lack of emotions
jokes.punchline: Questioning whether his one laugh even counts as emotion
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery that questions the basic humanity of laughter

jokes.text: Network the bejesus out of me. I want people to be sick of the sight of my face.
jokes.setup: Sue trying to network aggressively while furloughed
jokes.punchline: Her overly enthusiastic approach to self-promotion
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Sue's desperation manifesting as aggressive networking

jokes.text: Both: Roger Furlong. Thought that, too. So obvious, Furlong.
jokes.setup: Someone asks who's stirring up trouble
jokes.punchline: Gary and Amy simultaneously naming Furlong as the obvious culprit
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective moment showing how predictable Furlong's antagonism has become

jokes.text: Wow, it's like anal leakage.
jokes.setup: Gary commenting on how information keeps coming out
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate and gross analogy
jokes.explanation: Perfectly inappropriate Gary comparison that's both disgusting and accurate

jokes.text: Somebody bring me the chinless head of Roger Furlong.
jokes.setup: Selina's anger at Furlong's machinations
jokes.punchline: Her creative insult combining 'head' with his physical characteristic
jokes.explanation: Sharp Selina insult that's both creative and specific to the target

jokes.text: Get rid of it. Refuse it. / Okay, look, if the VP did have a window, it'd be a high one and she'd push you out of it.
jokes.setup: Selina telling Gary to get rid of a book
jokes.punchline: Gary's elaborate threat about what Selina would do if she had a window
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that turns a simple command into an elaborate hypothetical threat

jokes.text: You're pregnant? Oh, my God, I'm so sorry. I did not mean that.
jokes.setup: Gary doing his impression of Sue being harsh
jokes.punchline: Him accidentally being cruel to someone who's actually pregnant
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment where Gary's attempt at impersonation backfires spectacularly

jokes.text: Pickle me eels and tickle me belly 'cause I am off to merry London Town for a right fuckabout, eh?
jokes.setup: Jonah announcing he's going to London
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous fake British accent and made-up slang
jokes.explanation: Peak Jonah obnoxiousness with his terrible attempt at British slang

jokes.text: It is a city where women are literally drunk all day. And I am going to mind their gaps, my friend.
jokes.setup: Jonah describing London women
jokes.punchline: His crude pun on 'mind the gap'
jokes.explanation: Typical Jonah crude humor that fits his character but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: Well, cum-for-brains, the government is shut down, so all the passport offices are closed.
jokes.setup: Gary asking why Jonah needs Selina for passport renewal
jokes.punchline: Jonah's crude insult while explaining the obvious
jokes.explanation: Standard Jonah insult that's more crude than clever

jokes.text: Where's Sue? Where's Sue? Who's the new Sue? Me.
jokes.setup: Jonah looking for Sue to make an appointment
jokes.punchline: Gary declaring himself the new Sue
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Gary's desperation to maintain relevance

jokes.text: See, this is the problem with high-definition. You don't want to see a dick in hi-def.
jokes.setup: Watching Chung on TV
jokes.punchline: Selina's crude observation about HD TV making everything too clear
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor with Selina's characteristic crudeness

jokes.text: What? I'm not overpaid. I'm independently wealthy. All right? Sorry.
jokes.setup: TV discussing overpaid lawmakers
jokes.punchline: Selina's tone-deaf defense that misses the point entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Selina's complete disconnect from regular people

jokes.text: Ma'am, the stench at your home is gone. Thanks to me.
jokes.setup: TV talking about vanquishing the stench
jokes.punchline: Gary proudly taking credit for removing Selina's garbage
jokes.explanation: Gary's misguided attempt to be helpful that will clearly backfire

jokes.text: uppity princess in her perfumed palace
jokes.setup: Amy explaining how removing just Selina's garbage looks
jokes.punchline: Her alliterative description of how others will see Selina
jokes.explanation: Clever alliteration that perfectly captures the optics problem

jokes.text: Wait, how am I gonna know which bags are yours? Do you want to let me know some of the things that are in the bags?
jokes.setup: Gary needs to retrieve Selina's specific garbage bags
jokes.punchline: His awkward request for her to describe her trash contents
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment where Gary's helpfulness creates an impossibly awkward situation

jokes.text: Awkward silence and door closing after Gary's question
jokes.setup: Gary's inappropriate question about trash contents
jokes.punchline: The uncomfortable silence and need to speak privately
jokes.explanation: Perfect use of silence to show how inappropriate Gary's question was

jokes.text: Yeah, yeah, yeah. I mean, we've been having lunch and then we've been having sex. And not really cards so much. Just, you know, just, like, sex.
jokes.setup: Amy clarifying what she means by 'seeing' Andrew
jokes.punchline: Her awkward over-explanation that makes it more uncomfortable
jokes.explanation: Amy's characteristic awkwardness when trying to be direct about personal matters

jokes.text: You dump him overboard like the toxic waste he is. / Come on, Amy. It is just so good. / Okay, then you see... see where it goes. / No, I have to end it. / Yeah. But I can't.
jokes.setup: Amy giving contradictory advice about Andrew
jokes.punchline: The rapid back-and-forth showing Amy's complete inability to give consistent counsel
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Amy's complete inability to maintain a position

jokes.text: I don't actually have to touch the trash, do I? Yes, you've got to embrace it.
jokes.setup: Jonah asking about touching garbage
jokes.punchline: Gary's philosophical response about 'embracing' trash
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast between Jonah's squeamishness and Gary's earnestness

jokes.text: Are you threatening me, Pez head? Because we got a compactor that takes shit and turns it into cubes.
jokes.setup: Gary trying to be official about government business
jokes.punchline: Walker's casual threat while insulting Jonah's appearance
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with a memorable threat and specific insult

jokes.text: Why don't you offer to blow him over by the recycled glass? It would throw up some interesting light effects. That would be romantic, wouldn't it?
jokes.setup: Gary saying they need to offer Walker something
jokes.punchline: Jonah's crude suggestion with artistic justification
jokes.explanation: Typical Jonah crudeness but with an unexpectedly artistic angle

jokes.text: They get so hot for it, I call it the Wet Wing. / Okay, that's disrespectful. / It's a building, Gary. It doesn't have feelings. / It has a spirit. / No, it doesn't.
jokes.setup: Jonah's crude joke about West Wing tours
jokes.punchline: Gary's sincere belief that the building has spiritual significance
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast between Jonah's cynicism and Gary's reverence

jokes.text: Vice president applauds shit compaction.
jokes.setup: Walker wanting VP to visit for business purposes
jokes.punchline: His imagined headline reducing the VP to garbage endorser
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd headline that perfectly captures how low the VP has fallen

jokes.text: Yeah, and I'm just talking about the 1970s. / I'm not. I was thinking of the 18th century.
jokes.setup: Discussion of historical discomfort
jokes.punchline: Selina's joke about the 70s being misunderstood by the Speaker
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke that shows the generational/cultural gap

jokes.text: Slide to unlock. Selina Meyer is a feisty operator and a fine VP.
jokes.setup: Speaker reading POTUS quote without inflection
jokes.punchline: Starting with a random phone interface instruction
jokes.explanation: Absurd detail that suggests the Speaker is literally reading from his phone

jokes.text: 'Slide to unlock' - Speaker reading phone screen instead of presidential quote
jokes.setup: Speaker preparing to read serious presidential statement
jokes.punchline: He reads his phone's lock screen first
jokes.explanation: Perfect moment of technology interfering with political gravitas

jokes.text: Fine and successful are synonyms. / You're grasping at straws. / I'm not grasping for straws.
jokes.setup: Selina insisting POTUS called her successful
jokes.punchline: Her desperate logic and correction of the idiom
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's denial and verbal confusion under pressure

jokes.text: If you die within the next six years, I will be attending your funeral and giving a eulogy when POTUS sends me there in his stead. And it is gonna be full of subtext. Chock-full of subtext.
jokes.setup: Selina being dismissed by the Speaker
jokes.punchline: Her elaborate threat about his future funeral
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that's both darkly funny and perfectly in character - one of the episode's best moments

jokes.text: Well, I look forward to that. Oh, wait, I'll be dead.
jokes.setup: Selina's elaborate funeral threat
jokes.punchline: Speaker's perfectly deadpan response
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that deflates Selina's elaborate threat

jokes.text: So what you're saying is your career has stalled. So now you and your slack pussy want to make big bucks as an oil lobbyist, right?
jokes.setup: Dan trying to spin being furloughed as opportunity
jokes.punchline: Sidney's brutal assessment of Dan's situation
jokes.explanation: Harsh but accurate assessment that cuts through Dan's spin

jokes.text: Great. I'm gonna have the most expensive starter, the most expensive entrée, and a $200 bottle of champagne. / I think I'm gonna keep it light. I'm gonna go chicken salad and a Diet Coke. / Nonsense. Give him exactly what I'm having. And we will split the champagne. It's our first date.
jokes.setup: Sidney ordering expensively on Dan's dime
jokes.punchline: Forcing Dan to match his order and calling it a 'first date'
jokes.explanation: Good power play showing Sidney establishing dominance over Dan

jokes.text: Or he tried to fuck the bear. Like Grizzly Adams. / Enjoying yourselves? / We were. / Are you laughing at the violent death of a young man? / Yeah, pretty much.
jokes.setup: Staff joking about bear attack victim
jokes.punchline: Their honest admission when called out
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that perfectly captures the callousness of political operatives

jokes.text: There's not a happy bear in show business. Or he tried to fuck the bear.
jokes.setup: Discussion about bears not liking to dance
jokes.punchline: Escalation to the most inappropriate possible explanation
jokes.explanation: Crude escalation that gets shock laugh but lacks sophistication. The show business line is better.

jokes.text: Forgot my noise-cancelling headphones. Guess what I just heard. I'm not here. Ergo, my ears aren't here either.
jokes.setup: Kent being on furlough but present
jokes.punchline: His logical but absurd reasoning about his ears
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent logic - technically correct but completely missing the point

jokes.text: This ass is closed for business. This ass is in clench-down. I don't want to be a decoy. Let the president take it in the ass. He might like it.
jokes.setup: Kent explaining VP traditionally takes blame
jokes.punchline: Selina's escalating refusal with crude wordplay
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with creative crude metaphors and perfect character voice

jokes.text: Here, the VP will be the lobster. Amy, greens. We'll call Mike carrots. Dan, you can be shrimp. Do you see what I did there, Dan? I made you shrimp.
jokes.setup: Sidney creating food code names
jokes.punchline: His obvious insult of calling Dan 'shrimp' and explaining it
jokes.explanation: Simple insult humor that works because Sidney explains his own joke

jokes.text: Boy, I just wish I ordered a plate of useless assholes. But you go to war with the army you have, right?
jokes.setup: Sidney's food metaphor system
jokes.punchline: His wish for a food that matches his assessment of Dan's colleagues
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the food metaphor with a harsh assessment

jokes.text: Well, tap your ruby slippers together and wake the fuck up. There is no job, Dorothy.
jokes.setup: Dan expressing interest in the Nebraska job
jokes.punchline: Sidney's Wizard of Oz reference revealing it was all fake
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that delivers the crushing revelation effectively

jokes.text: So why don't you take your little vanilla Thriller dance over to Danny Chung? He's the shiny turd rising to the top of the bowl right now.
jokes.setup: Sidney dismissing Dan
jokes.punchline: His elaborate insults combining dance reference and scatological metaphor
jokes.explanation: Creative combination of insults that paints a vivid and disgusting picture

jokes.text: When I said fault, I meant that it wasn't your husband's fault because bears are naturally hungry. Does that make sense? Hello? Hello?
jokes.setup: Mike trying to console the bear widow
jokes.punchline: His terrible logic making things worse, then getting hung up on
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment showing Mike's complete inability to handle sensitive situations

jokes.text: Oh, please. Please. You think this is bad? Wait till you get to London. Everything smells like urine. Even the food.
jokes.setup: Jonah dealing with garbage smell
jokes.punchline: His gross generalization about London
jokes.explanation: Typical Jonah gross-out humor that fits his obnoxious character

jokes.text: The Chinese invented pizza. / No, they didn't. / Yeah, they did.
jokes.setup: Selina enjoying pizza
jokes.punchline: Roger's confident but wrong historical claim
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment showing Roger's tendency to make authoritative but wrong statements

jokes.text: That's as much a fact as Will's impotence. / I don't think that's appropriate. / He doesn't mind. Do you, Will? / Whatever works, sir.
jokes.setup: Roger making a comparison
jokes.punchline: His casual reference to Will's intimate problem and Will's resigned acceptance
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Roger's inappropriate oversharing and Will's defeated acceptance

jokes.text: I would have guessed Old Maid. / Want to play Go Fish? / Sure.
jokes.setup: Selina saying she likes Shanghai (card game)
jokes.punchline: Roger's subtle insult about her being an old maid
jokes.explanation: Subtle insult disguised as a card game preference

jokes.text: Yeah, you can start calling me the Hurty Dirty Man. / Yeah, I'm not gonna call you that. / All right. / I'm not.
jokes.setup: Roger describing his dirt on people
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous self-appointed nickname and Selina's firm refusal
jokes.explanation: Absurd nickname that Roger clearly thought was clever, shot down immediately

jokes.text: It's like a sudden monsoon in Goa, sir. / Sudden monsoon. They predict those things weeks in advance, you idiot.
jokes.setup: Will trying to make a poetic comparison
jokes.punchline: Roger's factual correction that ruins the metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Will's attempt at eloquence with practical knowledge

jokes.text: I know, it's hard to believe that they have female FBI agents. / Oh! / I'm kidding. I'm kidding. I'm kidding. / I love that kind of misogyny. / I love misogyny like that. / Yeah, it's the best kind.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Silence of the Lambs
jokes.punchline: Roger's offensive joke and Selina's enthusiastic appreciation of 'good' misogyny
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing both characters' problematic attitudes disguised as appreciation for 'quality' bigotry

jokes.text: We're gonna be the shitty Sopranos.
jokes.setup: Loading garbage bags into a car
jokes.punchline: Gary's comparison to the famous mob show
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective pop culture reference that captures their pathetic criminal-like activity

jokes.text: It's bigger than your mother's womb and you were in there till you were 15. / Oh, my God.
jokes.setup: Jonah complaining about fitting in the car
jokes.punchline: Sue's brutal insult about his birth and development
jokes.explanation: Excellent Sue insult that's both creative and devastatingly specific to Jonah

jokes.text: But heroes don't get to choose, they get chosen.
jokes.setup: Dan advising Chung about the widow situation
jokes.punchline: His pseudo-profound statement about heroism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan pseudo-wisdom that sounds profound but is actually empty political speak

jokes.text: Oval Office ordering a pizza and an air strike. Thin crust, extra warheads.
jokes.setup: Dan fantasizing about working with Chung as president
jokes.punchline: His casual juxtaposition of pizza and military action
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Dan's casual attitude toward power and violence

jokes.text: You know, I didn't realize how good I was at this job until someone else did it so badly. / Different isn't bad. / No, bad is bad.
jokes.setup: Selina commenting on Gary's replacement
jokes.punchline: Her blunt correction of Gary's diplomatic response
jokes.explanation: Sharp Selina putdown that rejects diplomatic niceties for brutal honesty

jokes.text: Gary, they don't even walk on the moon anymore. They're basically a bunch of nerds who work in a hangar.
jokes.setup: Gary expressing disappointment about missing NASA meeting
jokes.punchline: Selina's dismissive assessment of modern NASA
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about NASA's diminished prestige

jokes.text: 'Gary, they don't even walk on the moon anymore. They're basically a bunch of nerds who work in a hangar.'
jokes.setup: Gary disappointed about missing NASA meeting
jokes.punchline: Selina's dismissive reduction of NASA
jokes.explanation: Selina's pragmatic deflation of romantic notions about space program

jokes.text: Go, period, fuck, period, yourself, exclamation point.
jokes.setup: Jonah asking about passport help
jokes.punchline: Selina's punctuation-specified insult
jokes.explanation: Creative insult format that's both funny and shows Selina's precision even in anger

jokes.text: Oh, you helped with the... with the garbage? Yeah, I helped with the garbage.
jokes.setup: Jonah claiming he helped with garbage
jokes.punchline: Selina's building rage at this pathetic justification
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Selina's incredulous anger building

jokes.text: So Sue's the new Gary and you are still the Amy. / Yeah. / Yeah.
jokes.setup: Selina explaining the new arrangements
jokes.punchline: Amy's resigned acceptance of her unchanged status
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Amy's resignation to her unchanging role

jokes.text: She is furloughing indiscriminately into the crowd.
jokes.setup: Amy asking what she missed
jokes.punchline: Dan's description of Selina's random firing spree
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor comparing furloughs to indiscriminate shooting

jokes.text: Oh, well, what could possibly go wrong?
jokes.setup: Learning about Selina's deal with Furlong and antidepressant use
jokes.punchline: Dan's sarcastic assessment of the situation
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic response that effectively captures the situation's disaster potential

jokes.text: That's precisely what I want to do, Dan. / Good. / Oh, okay, then, yes. Exactly.
jokes.setup: Dan suggesting First Response show
jokes.punchline: The confusion about whether this is good or bad advice
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the confusion about what constitutes good advice

jokes.text: Ma'am, I-I just shovel it. I don't account for it.
jokes.setup: Selina asking complex waste management questions
jokes.punchline: Worker's simple, honest response about his actual job
jokes.explanation: Perfect working-class response that deflates political pontificating with simple honesty

tmdb_overview: With D.C. in the midst of a government shutdown, Selina is forced to furlough some of her staff. A damaging story leaks about Selina’s ex-husband, Andrew; Danny Chung takes advantage of the shutdown; Gary Jonah go on a run to retrieve Selina’s trash.
insight: Political gridlock spawns 68 jokes in 44 minutes, but impact never matches density.
